{{/* Renders one CRD type page (product: crd). The page's plural param selects the definition from site.Data.apis.crds; the example comes from the validated manifest at examples/reference/.yaml. The header leads with the kind, a description and a link to its concept doc, then an apiVersion/kind/scope strip; spec and status follow, with deep objects promoted to their own named sections (apiBuilder/collectTypes + fieldList). The H1 is in single-list.html. */}} {{ $plural := .Params.plural }} {{ $crd := "" }} {{ with index site.Data.apis.crds $plural }}{{ $crd = .definition }}{{ end }} {{ with $crd }} {{ $kind := .spec.names.kind }} {{ $group := .spec.group }} {{ $scope := .spec.scope }} {{ $shorts := .spec.names.shortNames }} {{ range $versionItem := .spec.versions }} {{ if $versionItem.served }} {{ $version := $versionItem.name }} {{ $schema := $versionItem.schema.openAPIV3Schema }} {{ $specSchema := index $schema.properties "spec" }} {{ $statusSchema := index $schema.properties "status" }} {{ $exampleRes := resources.Get (printf "examples/reference/%s.yaml" $plural) }}
{{/* Lead description (schema, else the concept doc's) + link to it. */}} {{ $concept := "" }} {{ with index site.Data.apigroups.concepts $kind }}{{ $concept = site.GetPage . }}{{ end }} {{ $lead := $.Params.description }} {{ if not $lead }}{{ with $concept }}{{ $lead = .Description }}{{ end }}{{ end }} {{ with $lead }}

{{ . | markdownify }}

{{ end }} {{ with $concept }}

Concept guide: {{ .Title }} →

{{ end }} {{/* The CRD's own subsections (Metadata / Example / Spec / Status) are lighter headings so they read as nested under the resource above, distinct from the embedded-object sections below. */}}

#Metadata

API version
{{ $group }}/{{ $version }}
Kind
{{ $kind }}
Scope
{{ $scope }}
{{ with $shorts }}
Short names
{{ range . }}{{ . }} {{ end }}
{{ end }}
{{ with $exampleRes }}

#Example

Manifest
{{ highlight .Content "yaml" "linenos=false" }}
{{ end }} {{/* Collect the promoted types reachable from spec/status (deduped, in reference order) so we can render one named section per type. */}} {{ $types := newScratch }} {{ with $specSchema }}{{ partial "apiBuilder/collectTypes" (dict "props" .properties "path" "spec" "kind" $kind "scratch" $types) }}{{ end }} {{ with $statusSchema }}{{ partial "apiBuilder/collectTypes" (dict "props" .properties "path" "status" "kind" $kind "scratch" $types) }}{{ end }} {{ with $specSchema }}

#Spec

{{ with .description }}

{{ . | markdownify }}

{{ end }} {{ partial "apiBuilder/fieldList" (dict "props" .properties "required" .required "path" "spec" "kind" $kind) }} {{ end }} {{ with $statusSchema }}{{ if .properties }}

#Status

{{ partial "apiBuilder/fieldList" (dict "props" .properties "required" .required "path" "status" "kind" $kind) }} {{ end }}{{ end }} {{/* Subresources the API server exposes for this kind (scale, status). */}} {{ with $versionItem.subresources }}

#Subresources

{{ range $sub, $cfg := . }}
# {{ $sub }} subresource
{{ if eq $sub "scale" }}

A scale subresource, so kubectl scale and replica-based autoscalers can drive it.

{{ else if eq $sub "status" }}

The status subresource is enabled; status is updated independently of spec.

{{ end }}
{{ if eq $sub "scale" }}
# specReplicasPath {{ $cfg.specReplicasPath }}

JSONPath to the desired replica count in spec.

# statusReplicasPath {{ $cfg.statusReplicasPath }}

JSONPath to the observed replica count in status.

{{ end }}
{{ end }}
{{ end }} {{/* Embedded objects, each its own top-level object section (peers of the CRD), tagged and set off by a rule above so they don't read as subsections of the resource. Fields keep their original path so nested promoted refs still resolve. */}} {{ range $name := $types.Get "order" }} {{ $node := index ($types.Get "nodes") $name }}

#{{ $name }} object

{{ with $node.desc }}

{{ . | markdownify }}

{{ end }} {{ partial "apiBuilder/fieldList" (dict "props" $node.props "required" $node.required "path" $node.path "kind" $kind) }} {{ end }}
{{/* .api-ref */}} {{ end }} {{ end }} {{ end }}
